registry.description: Despair is the collapse of hope — the inner state of the person who has stopped believing that things can be otherwise. The biblical vocabulary includes a Hebrew term that literally means not faithful, the failure of the capacity to trust, alongside images of devastation and the shattering of expectation. Despair is the most complete form of hopelessness: not just sadness but the extinction of the conviction that goodness is possible or that God is present. The psalms are unflinching in naming it.

terms.medium_def:
1) to despair
1a) (Niphal) to be despaired, be desperate
1b) (Piel) to cause to despair
interj
2) it is hopeless!
terms.decision_reason: F4: parent H2976 in anchor list, section_type=primary

And you have praised the gods of silver and gold , of bronze , iron , wood , and stone , which do not see or hear or know , but the God in whose hand is your breath , and whose are all your ways , you have not honored .

verses.esv_text: Dan 6:12 Then they came near and said before the king , concerning the injunction , “O king ! Did you not sign an injunction , that anyone who makes petition to any god or man within thirty days except to you, O king , shall be cast into the den of lions ?” The king answered and said , “The thing stands fast , according to the law of the Medes and Persians , which cannot be revoked .”

terms.medium_def:
A location first mentioned at Jos.13.26; 
referred to as Lo-debar (לֹא דְבָר), or Debir (לִדְבִר). § a town in Manasseh in Gilead east of the Jordan

terms.medium_def:
1) to support, confirm, be faithful
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to support, confirm, be faithful, uphold, nourish
1a1a) foster-father (subst.)
1a1b) foster-mother, nurse
1a1c) pillars, supporters of the door
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to be established, be faithful, be carried, make firm
1b1a) to be carried by a nurse
1b1b) made firm, sure, lasting
1b1c) confirmed, established, sure
1b1d) verified, confirmed
1b1e) reliable, faithful, trusty
1c) (Hiphil)
1c1) to stand firm, to trust, to be certain, to believe in
1c1a) stand firm
1c1b) trust, believe
Aramaic equivalent: a.man (אֲמַן "to trust" H0540)

terms.medium_def:
: faithful
1) firmness, faithfulness, truth
1a) sureness, reliability
1b) stability, continuance
1c) faithfulness, reliableness
1d) truth
1d1) as spoken
1d2) of testimony and judgment
1d3) of divine instruction
1d4) truth as a body of ethical or religious knowledge
1d5) true doctrine
adv
2) in truth, truly

verses.esv_text: Psa 39:12 “ Hear my prayer , O Lord , and give ear to my cry ; hold not your peace at my tears ! For I am a sojourner with you, a guest , like all my fathers .

Psa 39:12 “Hear my prayer, O Lord,

hold not your peace at my tears!

a guest, like all my fathers.

verses.esv_text: Psa 46:10 “Be still , and know that I am God . I will be exalted among the nations , I will be exalted in the earth !”

Psa 46:10 “Be still, and know that I am God.
I will be exalted among the nations,

verses.esv_text: Psa 50:7 “ Hear , O my people , and I will speak ; O Israel , I will testify against you. I am God , your God .

Psa 50:7 “Hear, O my people, and I will speak;
O Israel, I will testify against you.
I am God, your God.

verses.esv_text: Rut 2:10 Then she fell on her face , bowing to the ground , and said to him, “ Why have I found favor in your eyes , that you should take notice of me , since I am a foreigner ?”
verses.preview_html: Rut 2:10 Then she fell on her face, bowing to the ground, and said to him, “Why have I found favor in your eyes, that you should take notice of me, since I am a foreigner?”

verses.esv_text: Rut 2:13 Then she said , “I have found favor in your eyes , my lord , for you have comforted me and spoken kindly to your servant , though I am not one of your servants .”
verses.preview_html: Rut 2:13 Then she said, “I have found favor in your eyes, my lord, for you have comforted me and spoken kindly to your servant, though I am not one of your servants.”

verses.esv_text: Rut 3:12 And now it is true that I am a redeemer . Yet there is a redeemer nearer than I .
verses.preview_html: Rut 3:12 And now it is true that I am a redeemer. Yet there is a redeemer nearer than I.

verses.esv_text: Rut 3:13 Remain tonight , and in the morning , if he will redeem you, good ; let him do it . But if he is not willing to redeem you, then, as the Lord lives , I will redeem you. Lie down until the morning .”
verses.preview_html: Rut 3:13 Remain tonight, and in the morning, if he will redeem you, good; let him do it. But if he is not willing to redeem you, then, as the Lord lives, I will redeem you. Lie down until the morning.”

verses.esv_text: Rut 3:9 He said , “ Who are you ?” And she answered , “ I am Ruth , your servant . Spread your wings over your servant , for you are a redeemer .”
verses.preview_html: Rut 3:9 He said, “Who are you?” And she answered, “I am Ruth, your servant. Spread your wings over your servant, for you are a redeemer.”

verses.esv_text: Rut 4:4 So I thought I would tell you of it and say , ‘ Buy it in the presence of those sitting here and in the presence of the elders of my people .’ If you will redeem it, redeem it. But if you will not , tell me, that I may know , for there is no one besides you to redeem it, and I come after you.” And he said , “ I will redeem it.”

verses.esv_text: Zec 11:16 For behold , I am raising up in the land a shepherd who does not care for those being destroyed , or seek the young or heal the maimed or nourish the healthy , but devours the flesh of the fat ones , tearing off even their hoofs .
verses.preview_html: Zec 11:16 For behold, I am raising up in the land a shepherd who does not care for those being destroyed, or seek the young or heal the maimed or nourish the healthy, but devours the flesh of the fat ones, tearing off even their hoofs.

verses.esv_text: Zec 11:6 For I will no longer have pity on the inhabitants of this land , declares the Lord . Behold , I will cause each of them to fall into the hand of his neighbor , and each into the hand of his king , and they shall crush the land , and I will deliver none from their hand .”
verses.preview_html: Zec 11:6 For I will no longer have pity on the inhabitants of this land, declares the Lord. Behold, I will cause each of them to fall into the hand of his neighbor, and each into the hand of his king, and they shall crush the land, and I will deliver none from their hand.”

verses.esv_text: Zec 12:2 “ Behold , I am about to make Jerusalem a cup of staggering to all the surrounding peoples . The siege of Jerusalem will also be against Judah .
verses.preview_html: Zec 12:2 “Behold, I am about to make Jerusalem a cup of staggering to all the surrounding peoples. The siege of Jerusalem will also be against Judah.

I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.esv_text: Dan 2:23 To you , O God of my fathers , I give thanks and praise , for you have given me wisdom and might , and have now made known to me what we asked of you , for you have made known to us the king’s matter .”
verses.preview_html:
Dan 2:23 To you, O God of my fathers,
I give thanks and praise,
for you have given me wisdom and might,
and have now made known to me what we asked of you,
for you have made known to us the king’s matter.”

verses.esv_text: Dan 2:30 But as for me , this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living , but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king , and that you may know the thoughts of your mind .
verses.preview_html: Dan 2:30 But as for me, this mystery has been revealed to me, not because of any wisdom that I have more than all the living, but in order that the interpretation may be made known to the king, and that you may know the thoughts of your mind.

verses.esv_text: Dan 2:8 The king answered and said , “ I know with certainty that you are trying to gain time , because you see that the word from me is firm —
verses.preview_html: Dan 2:8 The king answered and said, “I know with certainty that you are trying to gain time, because you see that the word from me is firm—

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:25 He answered and said , “ But I see four men unbound , walking in the midst of the fire , and they are not hurt ; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods .”
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:25 He answered and said, “But I see four men unbound, walking in the midst of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.”

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:18 This dream I , King Nebuchadnezzar , saw . And you , O Belteshazzar , tell me the interpretation , because all the wise men of my kingdom are not able to make known to me the interpretation , but you are able , for the spirit of the holy gods is in you .”
verses.preview_html: Dan 4:18 This dream I, King Nebuchadnezzar, saw. And you, O Belteshazzar, tell me the interpretation, because all the wise men of my kingdom are not able to make known to me the interpretation, but you are able, for the spirit of the holy gods is in you.”

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:30 and the king answered and said , “Is not this great Babylon , which I have built by my mighty power as a royal residence and for the glory of my majesty ?”
verses.preview_html: Dan 4:30 and the king answered and said, “Is not this great Babylon, which I have built by my mighty power as a royal residence and for the glory of my majesty?”

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:34 At the end of the days I , Nebuchadnezzar , lifted my eyes to heaven , and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High , and praised and honored him who lives forever , for his dominion is an everlasting dominion , and his kingdom endures from generation to generation ;
verses.preview_html:
Dan 4:34 At the end of the days I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High, and praised and honored him who lives forever,

for his dominion is an everlasting dominion,
and his kingdom endures from generation to generation;

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:37 Now I , Nebuchadnezzar , praise and extol and honor the King of heaven , for all his works are right and his ways are just ; and those who walk in pride he is able to humble .
verses.preview_html: Dan 4:37 Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and extol and honor the King of heaven, for all his works are right and his ways are just; and those who walk in pride he is able to humble.

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:4 I , Nebuchadnezzar , was at ease in my house and prospering in my palace .
verses.preview_html: Dan 4:4 I, Nebuchadnezzar, was at ease in my house and prospering in my palace.

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:7 Then the magicians , the enchanters , the Chaldeans , and the astrologers came in , and I told them the dream , but they could not make known to me its interpretation .
verses.preview_html: Dan 4:7 Then the magicians, the enchanters, the Chaldeans, and the astrologers came in, and I told them the dream, but they could not make known to me its interpretation.

verses.esv_text: Dan 4:9 “O Belteshazzar , chief of the magicians , because I know that the spirit of the holy gods is in you and that no mystery is too difficult for you, tell me the visions of my dream that I saw and their interpretation .

verses.esv_text: Dan 5:16 But I have heard that you can give interpretations and solve problems . Now if you can read the writing and make known to me its interpretation , you shall be clothed with purple and have a chain of gold around your neck and shall be the third ruler in the kingdom .”
